title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that as LLM capabilities commoditize, agent identity and continuity should be centered on session logs rather than on models, frameworks, or tools. He proposes that durable, portable append-only logs, not orchestration layers, should be the primitive for agent infrastructure, enabling durability, resumability, forkability, and portability across models and runtimes.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara argues that as LLM capabilities commoditize, agent identity and continuity should center on session logs rather than on models, frameworks, or tools. The core claim: a durable, portable append-only log, not the orchestration layer, should be the primitive for agent infrastructure. The takeaways are the properties such a log enables: durability, resumability, forkability, and portability across models and runtimes.
